executiveSummary.text: Broad risk tone remains constructive but less clean: S&P 500 +0.13%, Nasdaq +0.03%, Dow +0.45%, VIX 15.77 (-1.7%). Records are being held by rotation, not broad euphoria; AI semicap and scarcity assets are doing more work than software megacaps.
Rates are the constraint: 2Y 4.064%, 10Y 4.467%, 30Y 4.980%, DXY 99.22. Fed rhetoric is not one-way dovish—Cleveland Fed’s Hammack warned rates may need to rise if inflation does not cool.

AI supply-chain scarcity is the day’s strongest signal: AMAT +7.0%, LRCX +5.5%, KLAC +5.4%, ASML +4.7%, AVGO +4.7%, SNPS +3.3%, GRID +2.1%. Bloomberg/Brave results flagged the widest chip-vs-software divergence on record; treat this as positioning plus capex-cycle confirmation, not a new thesis by itself.

Hyperscaler funding risk surfaced: Alphabet fell -3.8% after announcing an $80B equity raise plan to fund AI build-out, with Berkshire investing $10B; MSFT also fell -4.2% amid software-to-semis rotation. This is dilution/ROI anxiety, not evidence that AI demand is slowing.

Scarcity commodities are mixed but miners ripped: WTI +1.8%, copper futures -0.4%, gold -0.5%, silver -1.2%, while COPX +4.0% and URNM +6.9%. Uranium strength is tied to Urenco’s fresh plan to expand US enrichment capacity nearly 50%, validating nuclear supply-chain urgency.
Singapore remains ballast: STI +1.18%; DBS/OCBC/UOB and major REIT quotes were flat on CNBC’s latest close, while DBS’s recent Asian wealth-centre expansion remains a structural fee-income positive.

usGlobal.what: US indices held near highs with low VIX despite rotation under the surface.
usGlobal.why: Semis/equipment/cyclicals offset pressure in software and Alphabet; CNBC reported the S&P closed at a fresh record.
usGlobal.implication: Base case is still risk-on, but breadth/leadership should be watched because leadership is narrowing toward AI infrastructure.

ratesMacro.what: Treasury yields edged up and the dollar was stable.
ratesMacro.why: Oil/inflation risk and Fed Hammack’s warning that rates may need to rise if inflation persists prevented a clean duration rally.
ratesMacro.implication: REITs and long-duration software need lower real yields; semicap earnings momentum can tolerate this better for now.

asiaSingapore.what: STI rose 1.18%; banks and REITs were mostly flat in available CNBC quotes.
asiaSingapore.why: Singapore remains a high-quality income/ballast sleeve; DBS’s latest Asian wealth expansion supports wealth-management fee growth over time.
asiaSingapore.implication: Keep SG dividend sleeve as ballast; add REIT risk only when US/Singapore yield pressure clearly fades.

commodities.what: Oil firmed, precious metals dipped, copper futures consolidated, but copper/uranium equities surged.
commodities.why: Middle East/oil risk supports energy inflation premia; uranium names reacted to Urenco’s US enrichment expansion; copper miners benefited from AI/power-grid scarcity narratives.
commodities.implication: Scarcity sleeve is working, but miner beta can overshoot spot commodities—rebalance only if position size breaks risk limits.

geopolitics.what: US-Iran/Middle East risk remains the clearest geopolitical transmission channel.
geopolitics.why: The path is oil/shipping insurance -> inflation expectations -> Treasury term premium/real yields -> pressure on long-duration equities and REITs.
geopolitics.implication: Do not assume “risk = gold up”; if the shock raises real yields/USD, gold can lag while oil and energy equities outperform.

moves.why: AI infrastructure capex rotation favored wafer-fab equipment after Nvidia/Computex commentary and industry reports of exceptional chip-vs-software divergence.
moves.implication: Confirms KL’s “own what AI depends on” thesis; second order is more demand for advanced packaging, metrology, deposition/etch and EUV capacity.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD only on 10–15% pullbacks or when earnings upgrades outpace multiple expansion. Invalidator: foundry capex cuts or order cancellations.

moves.why: Reuters reported Urenco will expand US uranium enrichment capacity nearly 50% backed by signed commitments; uranium equities priced nuclear fuel-chain urgency.
moves.implication: Positive for scarcity thesis but increases volatility; third order is policy support for nuclear as AI/data-center power demand strains grids.
moves.action: HOLD/WATCH; add only if spot/term uranium contracts confirm, not after a one-day squeeze. Invalidator: enrichment expansion reduces bottleneck pricing faster than demand grows.

moves.why: Copper/grid equities caught a scarcity bid even as copper futures slipped slightly; investors are paying for electrification and data-center power bottlenecks.
moves.implication: Good 2nd-order exposure to AI power demand; miners remain higher-beta than metal spot.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD on broad commodity/liquidity drawdowns, not chase days.

moves.why: Alphabet announced plans to raise $80B including a $10B Berkshire investment to fund AI build-out; broader software/hyperscaler names lagged semis as investors questioned dilution and AI ROI timing.
moves.implication: Not thesis damage if capex converts into cloud/search productivity; near-term risk is market forcing hyperscalers to prove returns while suppliers capture revenue first.
moves.action: HOLD/WATCH; add only if selloff deepens without deterioration in cloud growth/margins. Invalidator: sustained AI capex funded by equity/debt with no revenue acceleration.

moves.why: EDA/IP and custom silicon rallied with the chip infrastructure basket.
moves.implication: Positive read-through for financial-toll-road-like AI infrastructure: royalties/IP/custom silicon benefit from complexity and capex.
moves.action: HOLD; trim only if valuation gets extreme versus forward revisions.

thesisImpact.what: AI scarcity thesis strengthened, but monetization risk moved from suppliers to hyperscalers.
thesisImpact.implication: Best portfolio lens: stay barbelled—own bottlenecks, keep cash, avoid overconcentrating in companies forced to fund capex at unattractive terms.
thesisImpact.action: HOLD overall; selective ADD only on volatility.

narratives.title: AI capex is becoming a financing cycle, not just an earnings story
narratives.description: Alphabet’s $80B raise reframes AI as a balance-sheet and capital-market question. First order: dilution/funding pressure. Second order: equipment, EDA, memory and grid suppliers get paid before hyperscalers prove ROI. Third order: credit/equity markets become part of the AI supply chain.
narratives.source: Reuters/CNBC/Yahoo Finance via Brave Search, last 24h

narratives.title: Chip/software divergence
narratives.description: Bloomberg reported semiconductor stocks rallied while software fell, with the gap between subsectors the widest on record. This supports KL’s semicap sleeve but warns against assuming all “AI” equities are equal.

narratives.title: Nuclear fuel chain is now an AI power bottleneck narrative
narratives.description: Urenco’s US enrichment expansion validates demand for domestic nuclear fuel security. The market is translating AI electricity demand into uranium/enrichment optionality.

secondOrderEffects.text: If oil/geopolitical risk lifts inflation expectations faster than nominal growth, the second-order effect is higher term premium and real-yield pressure; that can hurt gold, REITs and software even during a geopolitical shock.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Watch real yields/DXY before adding GLD/REITs on headlines.

secondOrderEffects.text: Hyperscaler equity/debt issuance to fund AI capex transfers value toward suppliers in the short run.
secondOrderEffects.implication: AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/ASML/SNPS/AVGO can outperform even while GOOG/MSFT lag.

secondOrderEffects.text: Uranium enrichment expansion is bullish demand validation but may eventually ease the bottleneck it is pricing.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Hold URNM, but do not extrapolate one-day +7% moves linearly.

investment.thesis: Keep the AI scarcity barbell: semicap equipment + EDA/IP + grid/power + copper/uranium, funded by cash ballast rather than leverage.
investment.trigger: Add on 10–15% sleeve drawdowns with no order/capex deterioration, or when earnings revisions rise faster than price.
investment.whyNow: Market is separating AI suppliers from AI spenders; KL already owns the right bottleneck assets.
investment.invalidator: Foundry/hyperscaler capex cuts, HBM oversupply, or AI workloads failing to monetize.

investment.thesis: Maintain Singapore banks/dividend sleeve as SGD income ballast.
investment.trigger: Add modestly if STI/banks correct while credit quality and dividends remain intact.
investment.whyNow: High net worth FI plan benefits from low-volatility SGD cashflow while US assets are valuation-sensitive.
investment.invalidator: Sharp Singapore unemployment/credit cycle deterioration or NIM compression without fee-income offset.

trading.thesis: Do not chase semicap gap-ups; use staggered limit orders after volatility.
trading.trigger: SOXX/semicap basket pulls back 5–8% while 10Y does not break materially higher.
trading.whyNow: One-day moves in AMAT/LRCX/KLAC are large and sentiment is hot.
trading.invalidator: 10Y >4.65% with DXY breakout and broad risk-off.

trading.thesis: GOOG/MSFT weakness is watchable, not automatically buyable.
trading.trigger: Further 5–10% drawdown with unchanged cloud growth/margins and credible capex ROI disclosures.
trading.whyNow: Funding/ROI concerns are real but may overshoot if core businesses remain strong.
trading.invalidator: More equity-funded capex without revenue acceleration.

watchlist.thesis: Rates are the master risk variable for REITs, software, gold and portfolio duration.
watchlist.trigger: US 10Y closes below 4.30% for easing tailwind, or above 4.65% for risk reduction review.
watchlist.whyNow: Fed rhetoric and oil inflation keep yields sticky.
watchlist.invalidator: Growth shock that drives yields lower for bad reasons.

watchlist.thesis: Uranium miners may be entering a policy-backed momentum phase.
watchlist.trigger: Term uranium prices and utility contracting confirm equity move.
watchlist.whyNow: Urenco expansion shows real demand commitments.
watchlist.invalidator: Spot/term prices fail to follow equities.

riskWatch.name: AI capex funding/ROI risk
riskWatch.description: Alphabet’s $80B raise shows AI build-out may require external capital; supplier revenue can rise while hyperscaler returns are questioned.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep exposure diversified across suppliers and hyperscalers; add only on valuation resets.

riskWatch.name: Rates/oil inflation feedback loop
riskWatch.description: Middle East/oil risk can lift inflation expectations and term premia, pressuring REITs, software and gold despite “safe haven” narratives.
riskWatch.mitigation: Monitor 10Y/real yields/DXY; preserve cash dry powder.

riskWatch.name: Crowded scarcity trades
riskWatch.description: Semicap, copper and uranium moves are large; positioning can reverse quickly if liquidity tightens.
riskWatch.mitigation: Avoid chasing +5–7% days; rebalance if sleeve weights exceed plan.

keyEvents.text: Fed’s Hammack said rates may need to rise if inflation does not abate.
Alphabet plans an $80B equity raise for AI build-out, including $10B from Berkshire Hathaway.
keyEvents.source: Reuters/CNBC/Yahoo Finance, Jun 2 via Brave Search

keyEvents.text: Urenco USA will expand US uranium enrichment capacity by nearly 50%.
Semiconductor stocks outperformed software sharply; Bloomberg flagged record chip-vs-software divergence.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The regime is still risk-on, but leadership is scarcity-led and rates-constrained. Suppliers to AI are winning; some AI spenders are being asked to prove funding discipline and ROI.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold KL’s barbell. Do not chase semicap/uranium spikes; use cash to add only on disciplined pullbacks where thesis remains intact. Keep Singapore income ballast and watch 10Y/DXY as the master risk trigger.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore single-day portfolio mark-to-market noise unless it signals capex cuts, credit stress, or thesis impairment. Also ignore simplistic “geopolitical risk = gold up” takes without checking real yields and USD.
